text: This is Said Arfanian presenting video of robotic repair of canal of noxious. The patient is a twenty year old healthy woman complaining of the right groin bulge and discomfort with prominent bulge on the exam. On CT scan had inguinal hernia with canal of noxious that extends into labia.

After accessing the abdominal cavity, the surgeon identifies the partially reducible right-side hernia. The anatomy of the contralateral side appears normal, with clear visualization of anatomical structures, including the direct and indirect hernia areas related to the epigastric vessels. The surgeon begins creating a peritoneal flap using scissors with cautery, advancing the flap inferiorly and laterally towards the hernia defect. The inferior epigastric vessels are visible in the periperitoneal plane.

Upon reaching the defect, the surgeon releases the neck of the multilobulated cyst circumferentially. Using scissors and cautery, the cyst is partially reduced. The right arm is then switched to a needle holder for improved handling and to prevent rupture of the fragile cyst.

The surgeon completes the reduction of the cyst, which is connected to the surrounding tissue through the canal of nuke. The canal is dissected towards the plutonal side to prevent cyst rupture. After cyst removal, a permanent polypropylene mesh is placed over the defect and secured to the soft tissue using absorbable sutures.
The surgeon closes the paternal flap using a barbed suture, which is also utilized to close the defect at the neck of the cyst dissection.
